status.title: Operational Status
status.defaultDesc: Research subject has been registered under a network researcher but has not yet fulfilled protocol requirements.
status.pendingDesc: Research subject has satisfied temporal and funding requirements, and is awaiting laboratory administrator verification.
status.failedDesc: Research subject failed to meet requirements or researcher violated the research covenant during subject acquisition.
status.lockedDesc: Research subject profile is locked and no longer viable for experimentation.
status.rejectedDesc: Researcher profile has permanently failed to satisfy protocol requirements.
status.paidDesc: Research network compensation has already been disbursed for this subject's recruitment.

faqPage.title: Frequently Asked Laboratory Questions
faqPage.q1: 1. What is your research compensation structure?
faqPage.a1: We disburse $100 flat compensation per acquisition — this is one of the most generously compensated research network programs in existence!
faqPage.q2: 2. How long does it take for an acquisition to appear in my researcher profile?
faqPage.a2: It should materialize immediately upon data synchronization.
faqPage.q3: 3. I just recorded an acquisition. Where can I observe it?
faqPage.a3_step2: In the lower right quadrant you will observe the option to Inspect Research Network Portal.
faqPage.a3_step3: Here you can examine all pending and approved research compensations.
faqPage.q4: 4. I have identified a research subject requiring laboratory apparatus. How do I acquire a referral compensation?
faqPage.a4_step2: Navigate to the lower section to locate your research network link.
faqPage.a4_step3: Transmit this link to your identified subject requiring laboratory apparatus.
faqPage.a4_step4: When they activate that link and requisition a laboratory profile, you acquire compensation.
faqPage.q5: 5. I have been approved for the InterServer Research Network program. What are the next protocol steps?
faqPage.a5_step1: Authenticate identity at your research network profile to access InterServer marketing specimens by activating the Banners and Links tab.
faqPage.a5_step2: Select the marketing specimen you wish to deploy on your research portal.
faqPage.a5_step3: You will observe banner propagation codes on the right side of all banners; replicate and deploy this code to your portal.
faqPage.a5_step4: When a subject activates that link and acquires a laboratory apparatus, you receive compensation.
faqPage.q6: 6. Can I conduct PPC bidding experiments to promote InterServer?
faqPage.a6_line1: Affirmative, but do NOT bid on our institutional designation, variations of our institutional designation, misspellings, or any keyword containing a portion thereof including InterServer, InterServer Hosting.
faqPage.a6_line2: Focus experimental bidding on keywords such as: rapid hosting, PHP laboratory apparatus, WordPress specimen container.
faqPage.a6_line3: Contact the laboratory if you require assistance in synthesizing keyword hypotheses.
faqPage.a6_line4: Ensure InterServer, InterServer Hosting, InterServer.net, InterServer web hosting are designated as negative campaign parameters.
faqPage.q7: 7. I have recorded an acquisition. When will my compensation be disbursed?
faqPage.a7_line1: After 90 solar cycles a valid acquisition will transition from Default Protocol status to Awaiting Activation Protocol.
faqPage.a7_line2: Once the acquisition reaches Awaiting status, compensation will be disbursed during the first 7 cycles of each lunar month.
faqPage.q8: 8. I have a pending acquisition that did not receive compensation.
faqPage.a8_line1: There may be several explanations for this anomaly. Occasionally we require additional time to validate an acquisition specimen.
faqPage.a8_line2: A simple diagnostic step: verify that your compensation method has been properly configured.
faqPage.q9: 9. How long do acquisitions remain in pending status before laboratory approval?
faqPage.q10: 10. Can I utilize my research network link for self-referral on profiles I will deploy for personal laboratory use?
faqPage.a10: Negative. Any detected contact with a self-referred profile will result in automatic experiment termination.
faqPage.q11: 11. Why was my research compensation rejected?
faqPage.a11_intro: Rejected status indicates that the compensation has permanently failed laboratory validation protocols.
faqPage.a11_reasons: Possible causes for this laboratory anomaly include:
faqPage.a11_reason1: The research subject has previously registered with our laboratory.
faqPage.a11_reason2: An identical Monetary Transfer Device was detected on another researcher profile.
faqPage.a11_reason3: The system apparatus detected that you, the research network member, are also operating the subject's laboratory profile.
faqPage.a11_policy: Since our disbursement of $100 per acquisition often exceeds our recovered costs even after 1 annual cycle, we maintain strict laboratory protocols to identify referrals that fail to satisfy our research guidelines. If you are recruiting a colleague or subject, simply provide them a link or experimental coupon. Do not share Monetary Transfer Device credentials and do not operate the profile.
faqPage.a11_reviewTos: Also please review our research network covenant here:
faqPage.q12: 12. I am a laboratory designer/developer and will recruit research subjects. Is this protocol acceptable?
faqPage.a12_line1: This experimental approach is not compatible with our laboratory protocols (or any other research network program). Once cross-profile data matches are detected, research compensations are terminated.
faqPage.a12_line2: This safeguard exists to prevent the same researcher from self-recruiting and extracting compensation from our research network program.
faqPage.a12_line3: You may wish to consider hosting research subjects directly and billing them yourself using a platform such as PayPal. You can requisition a reseller laboratory apparatus from us to initiate operations.
faqPage.q13: 13. Where can I configure my compensation disbursement method?
faqPage.a13: You can configure your compensation method,
